title: What a strange time indeed — early-COVID monologue from Dad's living room
html:
What a strange time indeed — early-COVID monologue from Dad's living room

Source: 2020-04-07 Google Doc, written from Dad's living-room desk in Grants Pass with lo-fi beats playing. Three weeks into the COVID lockdown. 16-year-old Yahir running scenario thinking on the post-pandemic world while Gtano's is "completely closed" and the family income stream has flatlined.

TL;DR

  • Operating axiom — "It doesn't matter what happens to you, what matters is how you react" — Yahir's stated stance during the COVID shutdown.
  • The Gtano's collapse, in real time — "Gtano's is completely closed and the way back to getting a healthy stream of income is not fruitful. As I dreadfully and painfully assist, it feels like an empty road, a dead end." — first-person witness to the family business going under.
  • Stock market as response — "You have the companies, you have the outlook, but only $1000. It doesn't seem like a lot, but we will have to make do with what we got." The $1000 brokerage account as economic-agency proxy at 16.
  • Macro thesis — US debt-to-GDP at "%150" will worsen; recovery "could be felt generations down the line." New Deal / Great Depression analog explicit.
  • The Goliath-falls bottom line — "Goliaths who once ruled the world will simply crumble, and new winners will emerge. Maybe time for an epic redemption story." Cites Jack Chapple's post-WW2 framing.
  • The mission line — "My mission is to find out what those are, I already have few in mind." — earliest cataloged post pandemic future thinking articulation.

Pattern observed

This is the earliest cataloged Yahir-as-investor / Yahir-as-thesis-builder document in the corpus. At 16, mid-COVID, watching Gtano's fail in real time, he pivots from passive-witness to active-thesis: read the macro, find the new winners, deploy $1000. The exact thesis-building pattern that becomes Corvallis Crypto Club in 2022 and Rankett in 2025 is already operational here. The note is the proto-form of post pandemic future thinking — the recurring frame of "what new patterns are getting installed under cover of crisis."

The RG3 "undercooked bat" joke and the lo-fi-beats / ChilledCow scene-setting place this firmly in 2020 internet-culture. Notably, the Dad wisdom thread is visually present (the document is being written at his desk while he's losing his business) but Dad is unnamed in the doc — Yahir is observing, not yet narrating, the family rupture that will dominate later notes (the 2022 Christmas Eve psychosis, the mi engaño arc).

Key quotes

  • "Things in our normal life will stay the same, concurrently, things in our normal life will become our 'new' normal life."
  • "With great strains on the economy and overall disruption of ecosystems comes massive change."
  • "My mission is to find out what those are."

Open questions

  • What were the "few in mind" post-pandemic winners Yahir was watching at 16? (Probably Tesla / crypto / e-commerce — but no specifics in this doc.)
  • Did the $1000 actually get deployed, and what return did it produce? (Cf. later crypto entries.)
  • This is the first Gtano's-collapse first-person account — does any earlier note narrate it?
